markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Rank candidates using evidence quality, graph proximity, relationship type, source authority, recency, and hub suppression. This MT belongs to RetrievalContextAndRanking. Retrieve project knowledge through explainable direct, graph, hybrid, and passage fallback modes.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Retrieval must choose explicit modes: none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag, or passage_fallback, with non_hybrid_reason where applicable.
Context bundles must be bounded, cited, explainable, restartable, and inspectable through backend and visual-debug surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: StructureAwareRanking leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: StructureAwareRanking is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 2bec339d; RetrievalContextAndRanking: 46 unit + 10 real-PG tests pass; cheapest-authoritative planner records mode+skip-reason in replayable RetrievalTrace; reuses MT-060 trace/bundle substrate (1 new migration 0260); MT-129 additive QueryRetrievalMode extension; folded-stub bridges (139-142) preserve ProjectBrain/SemanticCatalog/AIReady/ContextPack intent as backend contracts.

evidence.finding: Ranking had no integration coverage; integrated graph/passage->rank->compile path unproven
evidence.fix: executor builds CandidateFeatures from EXECUTED graph edges (confidence/proximity/edge-type/hub) or fallback passages (extraction confidence) and feeds rank_candidates output through snippets into the compiler; TraversedEdge now carries edge confidence
evidence.tests: knowledge_retrieval_pipeline_tests 12 passed incl. executed_pipeline_graph_path_ranks_and_compiles (defines>mentions ranking over real traversed edges, span citations, persisted bundle+trace) + executed_pipeline_falls_back_to_passages_ranks_and_compiles (passage ranking by confidence)

evidence.finding: trace trust_adjust sentinel (-1.0) misreported the actual hub penalty (-0.25)

findings.title: ranking has no integration coverage; the integrated graph/passage -> rank -> compile path is unproven
findings.evidence: rank_candidates (ranking.rs:103) is unit-tested only; the compiler PG test (pipeline_tests:253) feeds hand-built BundleCandidates, so real candidates are never ranked before compile. The end-to-end claim in the test header overstates.
findings.fix: Add a test that ranks real graph/passage candidates and feeds the ranked output into the compiler.

findings.title: trace trust_adjust sentinel misreports the hub penalty
findings.evidence: ranking.rs:128 sets scores.trust_adjust = Some(-1.0) for a via-hub candidate, but the actual score adjustment applied is -hub_penalty (-0.25 default); the persisted trust_adjust value does not reflect the real adjustment.
findings.fix: Record the actual penalty applied (or rename the field) so the trace is truthful.

missing_tests: test: real candidates ranked then compiled (integrated rank->budget->bundle)

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-134 requires deterministic structure-aware ranking of retrieval candidates over evidence quality, graph proximity, relationship type, source authority, recency, and hub suppression with a stable tie-break, producing spec RetrievalCandidate objects persisted to a replayable RetrievalTrace. The code implements exactly this in ranking.rs and wires it into a real PostgreSQL-backed executed pipeline (executor.rs) proven by two integration tests that rank real graph edges and real fallback passages and persist the bundle+trace.
findings: ranking.rs:88-98 score_candidate combines all six MT-required axes (evidence_quality, graph_proximity, relationship_type_weight, source_authority, recency) with hub_penalty subtracted (suppressed not removed) and clamped to [0,1] — matches spec 02-system-architecture.md:11228 'score + select deterministically' and the named axes at :10813.

ranking.rs:103-151 rank_candidates pre-sorts by candidate_id then sorts by score desc with stable tie-break by candidate_id, emitting spec RetrievalCandidate objects with base_score + tiebreak (spec field confirmed at 02-system-architecture.md:11490 'tiebreak: string'). Determinism proven by unit test ranking_is_deterministic_with_stable_tiebreak (ranking.rs:209).

Relationship-type ordering is structural: CandidateFeatures::relationship_weight_for (ranking.rs:73-82) ranks defines/implements/validates (1.0) above mentions (0.25) — satisfies the 'relationship type' axis; asserted in relationship_weights_order_definitional_above_mentions and end-to-end in the graph test (defines edge 0.95 outranks mentions edge 0.5).

Adversarial-v2 LOW fix verified in code: ranking.rs:128-134 records the real hub penalty Some(-weights.hub_penalty) (-0.25) instead of the prior -1.0 sentinel; asserted in hub_candidate_is_penalized_but_present (ranking.rs:204-205, asserts Some(-0.25)).

Adversarial-v2 MEDIUM fix verified: executor.rs:89-300 execute_retrieval feeds EXECUTED graph edges (confidence/proximity/edge-type/hub at :200-228) or fallback passages (extraction confidence at :174-198) through rank_candidates (:230) into the compiler with persisted bundle+trace (:276-289). The integrated rank->compile path is now real, not hand-built candidates.

Real PostgreSQL proof confirmed (sub-rule 2): knowledge_memory_fixtures.rs header 'Real PostgreSQL only. No SQLite, no mocks'; fixture uses managed-PG auto-discovery via knowledge_pg_support and skip_if_no_pg! SKIPs loudly when PG absent. executed_pipeline_graph_path_ranks_and_compiles (pipeline_tests.rs:610-690) creates real edges, asserts ranked[0].base_score > ranked[1], persists bundle (2 span-cited items) + trace mode graph_traversal. executed_pipeline_falls_back_to_passages_ranks_and_compiles (:481-604) asserts the 0.9 passage outranks 0.4, persists bundle+trace with passage_fallback posture.

Sub-rule 1: no todo!/unimplemented!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to follow-on'/'pending MT-'/*Unavailable escapes in ranking.rs or executor.rs (grep clean). Documented feature defaults (GRAPH_SOURCE_AUTHORITY, NEUTRAL_RECENCY at executor.rs:54-56) are honest neutral constants for axes not yet per-record, not placeholder escapes — the ranking still runs and discriminates on the live axes.

Boundary/scope: all changes confined to src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_retrieval/ and tests/ (allowed_paths). No SQLite, no external search engine, no Docker, no forbidden surfaces touched. Aside (non-blocking): packet cites v02.192 spec anchors; resolved against current v02.194 where the same ranking clauses exist.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
